title: Formatting, Style, and Editor Consistency · Mastery Check
summary: Create one fast deterministic formatting path for code and configuration. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a zero-diff formatting gate. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose a deterministic formatter and define supported file scope.
Separate local write mode from CI check-only mode.
Use editor-independent whitespace, newline, and charset settings.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Formatter Choice evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Formatter Choice assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Choose a deterministic formatter and define supported file scope. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates format style. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Formatter Choice, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a zero-diff formatting gate.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Formatting preferences should be enforced manually in review. This fails the Formatter Choice contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Clang AddressSanitizer and UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer (Clang 23 development documentation) — Controls native-code runtime sanitizer purpose, build profiles, output, limitations, and security cautions.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose a deterministic formatter and define supported file scope.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Format Migration applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Introduce or replace formatting with isolated reviewable migration commits.
